• 1 EFA with clean data
    • 1.1 - Outliers
    • 1.2 - FWB items
  • 2 Generating Scales based on EFA results
  • 3 Reliability

Testing FWB Structure

1 EFA with clean data

setwd("/Users/lisafuetterer/Desktop/UNI/MASTER/MASTERARBEIT/Datenauswertung")

library(readxl)
data <- read_excel("data.xlsx", sheet = 1)

1.1 - Outliers

data_clean <- data[!data$MD_outlier, ]

library(psych)

allFWB_items <- c("FWB01", "FWB02", "FWB03", "FWB04", "FWB05", "FWB06", "FWB07", "FWB08", "FWB09", "FWB10","CBB01", "CBB02", "CBB03", "CBB04",                              "CBB05", "CBB06", "CBB07")

data_clean_FWB <- data_clean[, allFWB_items]

# Parallel Analysis (Horn, 1965)
fa.parallel(
  data_clean_FWB,
  fa   = "fa",    
  fm   = "ml",    
  main = "Parallel Analysis (raw data)"
)

## Parallel analysis suggests that the number of factors =  4  and the number of components =  NA
#MAP Test
R_FWB_clean <- cor(data_clean_FWB, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

vss_FWB_clean <- VSS(
  x     = R_FWB_clean,                 
  n.obs = nrow(data_clean_FWB),        
  n      = 10,                   
  rotate = "none",              
  fm     = "pa",                
  SMC    = FALSE                
)

print(vss_FWB_clean$map)  
##  [1] 0.01729108 0.01677256 0.02154690 0.02771258 0.03413138 0.04330828
##  [7] 0.05360715 0.06825372 0.08425362 0.10606072
which.min(vss_FWB_clean$map) 
## [1] 2
#Kaiser’s rule
eigenv_clean <- eigen(cor(data_clean_FWB, use="pairwise.complete.obs"))$values
round(eigenv_clean, 2)       
##  [1] 6.06 1.54 1.09 1.00 0.86 0.83 0.78 0.70 0.63 0.61 0.53 0.48 0.47 0.41 0.37
## [16] 0.34 0.29
sum(eigenv_clean > 1)         
## [1] 4
# alle Tests ergeben unterschiedliche Faktorzahlen

efa5 <- fa(
  r        = R_FWB_clean,
  nfactors = 5,
  fm       = "pa",
  rotate   = "promax"
)
## maximum iteration exceeded
## Loading required namespace: GPArotation
print(
  efa5,
  cut     = 0.30,    
  digits  = 2,       
  sort    = TRUE     
)
## Factor Analysis using method =  pa
## Call: fa(r = R_FWB_clean, nfactors = 5, rotate = "promax", fm = "pa")
## Standardized loadings (pattern matrix) based upon correlation matrix
##       item   PA4   PA1   PA5   PA2   PA3   h2   u2 com
## FWB03    3  0.70                         0.45 0.55 1.0
## FWB06    6  0.65                         0.38 0.62 1.3
## FWB10   10  0.58                         0.34 0.66 1.5
## FWB04    4  0.48                         0.41 0.59 1.3
## FWB01    1  0.48                         0.52 0.48 1.9
## FWB05    5  0.43  0.34                   0.47 0.53 2.2
## FWB08    8        0.91                   0.63 0.37 1.0
## FWB07    7  0.35  0.65                   0.66 0.34 1.6
## FWB09    9              1.03             0.77 0.23 1.1
## CBB07   17              0.77             0.56 0.44 1.1
## CBB03   13                               0.48 0.52 3.1
## CBB02   12                    0.65       0.48 0.52 1.1
## CBB05   15                    0.58       0.26 0.74 1.1
## CBB01   11        0.49        0.52       0.52 0.48 2.5
## CBB06   16                               0.25 0.75 3.5
## CBB04   14                               0.26 0.74 3.0
## FWB02    2                          0.80 0.75 0.25 1.2
## 
##                        PA4  PA1  PA5  PA2  PA3
## SS loadings           2.34 1.92 1.68 1.34 0.90
## Proportion Var        0.14 0.11 0.10 0.08 0.05
## Cumulative Var        0.14 0.25 0.35 0.43 0.48
## Proportion Explained  0.29 0.23 0.21 0.16 0.11
## Cumulative Proportion 0.29 0.52 0.73 0.89 1.00
## 
##  With factor correlations of 
##      PA4  PA1  PA5  PA2  PA3
## PA4 1.00 0.65 0.58 0.46 0.39
## PA1 0.65 1.00 0.72 0.55 0.48
## PA5 0.58 0.72 1.00 0.65 0.36
## PA2 0.46 0.55 0.65 1.00 0.26
## PA3 0.39 0.48 0.36 0.26 1.00
## 
## Mean item complexity =  1.7
## Test of the hypothesis that 5 factors are sufficient.
## 
## df null model =  136  with the objective function =  5.9
## df of  the model are 61  and the objective function was  0.31 
## 
## The root mean square of the residuals (RMSR) is  0.03 
## The df corrected root mean square of the residuals is  0.04 
## 
## Fit based upon off diagonal values = 0.99
## Measures of factor score adequacy             
##                                                    PA4  PA1  PA5  PA2  PA3
## Correlation of (regression) scores with factors   0.91 0.92 0.94 0.86 0.86
## Multiple R square of scores with factors          0.82 0.86 0.87 0.74 0.75
## Minimum correlation of possible factor scores     0.65 0.71 0.75 0.48 0.49
efa4 <- fa(
  r        = R_FWB_clean,
  nfactors = 4,
  fm       = "pa",
  rotate   = "promax"
)

print(
  efa4,
  cut     = 0.30,    
  digits  = 2,       
  sort    = TRUE     
)
## Factor Analysis using method =  pa
## Call: fa(r = R_FWB_clean, nfactors = 4, rotate = "promax", fm = "pa")
## Standardized loadings (pattern matrix) based upon correlation matrix
##       item   PA1   PA2   PA4   PA3   h2   u2 com
## FWB03    3  0.72                   0.45 0.55 1.0
## FWB06    6  0.70                   0.40 0.60 1.2
## FWB04    4  0.53                   0.40 0.60 1.2
## FWB01    1  0.53                   0.51 0.49 1.7
## FWB10   10  0.48                   0.24 0.76 1.1
## FWB05    5  0.45  0.40             0.48 0.52 2.1
## FWB02    2                         0.31 0.69 3.3
## FWB08    8        0.85             0.60 0.40 1.0
## FWB07    7  0.33  0.58             0.63 0.37 1.7
## CBB03   13        0.32             0.48 0.52 2.8
## FWB09    9              0.85       0.68 0.32 1.1
## CBB07   17              0.83       0.62 0.38 1.1
## CBB06   16                         0.22 0.78 2.6
## CBB02   12                    0.64 0.47 0.53 1.1
## CBB05   15                    0.60 0.26 0.74 1.1
## CBB01   11        0.50        0.53 0.52 0.48 2.4
## CBB04   14                         0.26 0.74 3.1
## 
##                        PA1  PA2  PA4  PA3
## SS loadings           2.45 2.16 1.62 1.32
## Proportion Var        0.14 0.13 0.10 0.08
## Cumulative Var        0.14 0.27 0.37 0.44
## Proportion Explained  0.33 0.29 0.21 0.17
## Cumulative Proportion 0.33 0.61 0.83 1.00
## 
##  With factor correlations of 
##      PA1  PA2  PA4  PA3
## PA1 1.00 0.66 0.56 0.49
## PA2 0.66 1.00 0.67 0.58
## PA4 0.56 0.67 1.00 0.65
## PA3 0.49 0.58 0.65 1.00
## 
## Mean item complexity =  1.7
## Test of the hypothesis that 4 factors are sufficient.
## 
## df null model =  136  with the objective function =  5.9
## df of  the model are 74  and the objective function was  0.45 
## 
## The root mean square of the residuals (RMSR) is  0.03 
## The df corrected root mean square of the residuals is  0.04 
## 
## Fit based upon off diagonal values = 0.99
## Measures of factor score adequacy             
##                                                    PA1  PA2  PA4  PA3
## Correlation of (regression) scores with factors   0.91 0.92 0.92 0.87
## Multiple R square of scores with factors          0.83 0.85 0.84 0.75
## Minimum correlation of possible factor scores     0.66 0.69 0.68 0.50
# efa5 und efa4 erscheint sinnlos, weil einzelne Faktoren nur ein item haben oder manche items auf keinem Faktor >30 laden
# außerdem lassen sie sich inhaltlich nicht erklären

efa2 <- fa(
  r        = R_FWB_clean,
  nfactors = 2,
  fm       = "pa",
  rotate   = "promax"
)

print(
  efa2,
  cut     = 0.30,    
  digits  = 2,      
  sort    = TRUE
)
## Factor Analysis using method =  pa
## Call: fa(r = R_FWB_clean, nfactors = 2, rotate = "promax", fm = "pa")
## Standardized loadings (pattern matrix) based upon correlation matrix
##       item   PA1   PA2   h2   u2 com
## FWB03    3  0.73       0.41 0.59 1.1
## FWB01    1  0.72       0.51 0.49 1.0
## FWB05    5  0.70       0.48 0.52 1.0
## FWB07    7  0.66       0.58 0.42 1.1
## FWB04    4  0.63       0.40 0.60 1.0
## FWB06    6  0.59       0.30 0.70 1.0
## FWB10   10  0.45       0.21 0.79 1.0
## FWB08    8  0.42       0.41 0.59 1.7
## FWB02    2  0.35       0.29 0.71 1.7
## CBB07   17        0.66 0.44 0.56 1.0
## CBB01   11        0.64 0.40 0.60 1.0
## CBB03   13        0.62 0.49 0.51 1.1
## FWB09    9        0.60 0.47 0.53 1.1
## CBB02   12        0.58 0.35 0.65 1.0
## CBB04   14        0.50 0.27 0.73 1.0
## CBB05   15        0.49 0.16 0.84 1.2
## CBB06   16        0.37 0.22 0.78 1.3
## 
##                        PA1  PA2
## SS loadings           3.48 2.91
## Proportion Var        0.20 0.17
## Cumulative Var        0.20 0.38
## Proportion Explained  0.54 0.46
## Cumulative Proportion 0.54 1.00
## 
##  With factor correlations of 
##      PA1  PA2
## PA1 1.00 0.66
## PA2 0.66 1.00
## 
## Mean item complexity =  1.1
## Test of the hypothesis that 2 factors are sufficient.
## 
## df null model =  136  with the objective function =  5.9
## df of  the model are 103  and the objective function was  0.89 
## 
## The root mean square of the residuals (RMSR) is  0.05 
## The df corrected root mean square of the residuals is  0.06 
## 
## Fit based upon off diagonal values = 0.98

## Correlation of (regression) scores with factors   0.93 0.92
## Multiple R square of scores with factors          0.87 0.84
## Minimum correlation of possible factor scores     0.74 0.68
#efa2 deutet auf Entfernung von FWB02 und CBB06

1.2 - FWB items

FWB_items_n <- c("FWB01", "FWB03", "FWB04", "FWB05", "FWB06", "FWB07", "FWB08", "FWB09", "FWB10","CBB01", "CBB02",                                                         "CBB03", "CBB04", "CBB05", "CBB07")
data_FWB_n <- data_clean[, FWB_items_n]

library(psych)

# Parallel Analysis
fa.parallel(
  data_FWB_n,
  fm       = "pa",     
  fa       = "fa",       
  main     = "Parallel-Analyse"
)

## Parallel analysis suggests that the number of factors =  4  and the number of components =  NA
# MAP Test
R_FWB_n <- cor(data_FWB_n, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

vss_FWB_n <- VSS(
  x     = R_FWB_n,                 
  n.obs = nrow(data_FWB_n),        
  n      = 10,                   
  rotate = "none",              
  fm     = "pa",                
  SMC    = FALSE                
)

print(vss_FWB_n$map)  
##  [1] 0.02017018 0.01945107 0.02587203 0.03222671 0.04295828 0.05840255
##  [7] 0.07880854 0.10550282 0.12587261 0.17225159
which.min(vss_FWB_n$map) 
## [1] 2
#Kaiser’s rule
eigenv_n <- eigen(cor(data_FWB_n, use="pairwise.complete.obs"))$values
round(eigenv_n, 2)        
##  [1] 5.55 1.52 1.08 0.87 0.80 0.75 0.70 0.62 0.60 0.53 0.49 0.45 0.40 0.34 0.30
sum(eigenv_n > 1)        
## [1] 3
efa2_n <- fa(
  data_FWB_n,
  nfactors = 2,
  rotate   = "promax",
  fm       = "pa"   
)

print(
  efa2_n,
  cut     = 0.30,   
  digits  = 2,      
  sort    = TRUE    
)
## Factor Analysis using method =  pa
## Call: fa(r = data_FWB_n, nfactors = 2, rotate = "promax", fm = "pa")
## Standardized loadings (pattern matrix) based upon correlation matrix
##       item   PA1   PA2   h2   u2 com
## FWB03    2  0.73       0.42 0.58 1.1
## FWB01    1  0.70       0.50 0.50 1.0
## FWB05    4  0.68       0.47 0.53 1.0
## FWB07    6  0.66       0.59 0.41 1.1
## FWB04    3  0.61       0.39 0.61 1.0
## FWB06    5  0.58       0.30 0.70 1.0
## FWB10    9  0.46       0.23 0.77 1.0
## FWB08    7  0.41  0.30 0.42 0.58 1.8
## CBB03   12        0.65 0.52 0.48 1.1
## CBB07   15        0.63 0.43 0.57 1.0
## CBB01   10        0.60 0.38 0.62 1.0
## FWB09    8        0.60 0.48 0.52 1.1
## CBB02   11        0.57 0.35 0.65 1.0
## CBB04   13        0.50 0.28 0.72 1.0
## CBB05   14        0.45 0.14 0.86 1.2
## 
##                        PA1  PA2
## SS loadings           3.23 2.65
## Proportion Var        0.22 0.18
## Cumulative Var        0.22 0.39
## Proportion Explained  0.55 0.45
## Cumulative Proportion 0.55 1.00
## 
##  With factor correlations of 
##      PA1  PA2
## PA1 1.00 0.63
## PA2 0.63 1.00
## 
## Mean item complexity =  1.1
## Test of the hypothesis that 2 factors are sufficient.
## 
## df null model =  105  with the objective function =  5.14 with Chi Square =  2082.97
## df of  the model are 76  and the objective function was  0.64 
## 
## The root mean square of the residuals (RMSR) is  0.05 
## The df corrected root mean square of the residuals is  0.06 
## 
## The harmonic n.obs is  412 with the empirical chi square  194.38  with prob <  2.5e-12 
## The total n.obs was  412  with Likelihood Chi Square =  260.05  with prob <  5.7e-22 
## 
## Tucker Lewis Index of factoring reliability =  0.871
## RMSEA index =  0.077  and the 90 % confidence intervals are  0.067 0.087
## BIC =  -197.55
## Fit based upon off diagonal values = 0.98
## Measures of factor score adequacy             
##                                                    PA1  PA2
## Correlation of (regression) scores with factors   0.93 0.91
## Multiple R square of scores with factors          0.86 0.83
## Minimum correlation of possible factor scores     0.72 0.66
# cross loadings auf FWB08
FWB_items_new <- c("FWB01", "FWB03", "FWB04", "FWB05", "FWB06", "FWB07", "FWB09", "FWB10","CBB01", "CBB02",                                      "CBB03",                    "CBB04", "CBB05", "CBB07")

data_FWB_new <- data_clean[, FWB_items_new]

library(psych)

# Parallel Analysis
fa.parallel(
  data_FWB_new,
  fm       = "pa",      
  fa       = "fa",     
  main     = "Parallel-Analyse"
)

## Parallel analysis suggests that the number of factors =  3  and the number of components =  NA
# MAP Test
R_FWB_new <- cor(data_FWB_new, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

vss_FWB_new <- VSS(
  x     = R_FWB_new,                 
  n.obs = nrow(data_FWB_new),        
  n      = 10,                   
  rotate = "none",              
  fm     = "pa",                
  SMC    = FALSE                
)

print(vss_FWB_new$map)  
##  [1] 0.02125859 0.01998221 0.02787140 0.03743159 0.05005743 0.06999726
##  [7] 0.09467432 0.12686172 0.17404698 0.22789953
which.min(vss_FWB_new$map) 
## [1] 2
#Kaiser’s rule
eigenv_new <- eigen(cor(data_FWB_new, use="pairwise.complete.obs"))$values
round(eigenv_new, 2)       
##  [1] 5.13 1.52 1.02 0.82 0.77 0.75 0.70 0.61 0.57 0.49 0.45 0.45 0.38 0.33
sum(eigenv_new > 1)        
## [1] 3
efa3_neu <- fa(
  data_FWB_new,
  nfactors = 3,
  rotate   = "promax",
  fm       = "pa"    # principal axis
)

# MAP und Kaiser's Kriterium deuten auf 3 Faktoren

print(
  efa3_neu,
  cut     = 0.30,  
  digits  = 2,      
  sort    = TRUE   
)
## Factor Analysis using method =  pa
## Call: fa(r = data_FWB_new, nfactors = 3, rotate = "promax", fm = "pa")
## Standardized loadings (pattern matrix) based upon correlation matrix
##       item   PA1   PA2   PA3   h2   u2 com
## FWB03    2  0.73             0.43 0.57 1.0
## FWB01    1  0.66             0.51 0.49 1.3
## FWB05    4  0.64             0.46 0.54 1.1
## FWB06    5  0.62             0.34 0.66 1.2
## FWB07    6  0.60             0.55 0.45 1.3
## FWB04    3  0.60             0.39 0.61 1.0
## FWB10    8  0.49             0.25 0.75 1.3
## FWB09    7        0.96       0.72 0.28 1.1
## CBB07   14        0.66       0.48 0.52 1.1
## CBB03   11        0.42       0.50 0.50 1.9
## CBB04   12        0.32       0.27 0.73 1.9
## CBB02   10              0.71 0.52 0.48 1.0
## CBB05   13              0.54 0.23 0.77 1.1
## CBB01    9              0.46 0.37 0.63 1.3
## 
##                        PA1  PA2  PA3
## SS loadings           2.83 1.95 1.26
## Proportion Var        0.20 0.14 0.09
## Cumulative Var        0.20 0.34 0.43
## Proportion Explained  0.47 0.32 0.21
## Cumulative Proportion 0.47 0.79 1.00
## 
##  With factor correlations of 
##      PA1  PA2  PA3
## PA1 1.00 0.62 0.52
## PA2 0.62 1.00 0.64
## PA3 0.52 0.64 1.00
## 
## Mean item complexity =  1.2
## Test of the hypothesis that 3 factors are sufficient.
## 
## df null model =  91  with the objective function =  4.46 with Chi Square =  1807.77
## df of  the model are 52  and the objective function was  0.26 
## 
## The root mean square of the residuals (RMSR) is  0.03 
## The df corrected root mean square of the residuals is  0.04 
## 
## The harmonic n.obs is  412 with the empirical chi square  69.75  with prob <  0.051 
## The total n.obs was  412  with Likelihood Chi Square =  105.83  with prob <  1.5e-05 
## 
## Tucker Lewis Index of factoring reliability =  0.945
## RMSEA index =  0.05  and the 90 % confidence intervals are  0.036 0.064
## BIC =  -207.26
## Fit based upon off diagonal values = 0.99
## Measures of factor score adequacy             
##                                                    PA1  PA2  PA3
## Correlation of (regression) scores with factors   0.92 0.92 0.86
## Multiple R square of scores with factors          0.85 0.85 0.74
## Minimum correlation of possible factor scores     0.69 0.70 0.47
fa.diagram(
  efa3_neu,
  main   = "EFA (PAF, 3 Faktoren)",
  cut    = .30,
  sort   = TRUE
)

2 Generating Scales based on EFA results

items_cog <- c("FWB03", "FWB01", "FWB05", "FWB06", "FWB07", "FWB04", "FWB10")
items_behav <- c("FWB09", "CBB07", "CBB03", "CBB04")
items_emo <- c("CBB02", "CBB05", "CBB01")

data_clean$Cog <-  rowMeans(data_clean[, items_cog])
data_clean$Behav <-  rowMeans(data_clean[, items_behav])
data_clean$Emo <-  rowMeans(data_clean[, items_emo])

hist(
  data_clean$Cog, 
  breaks = 20,
  col    = "#99DCBD",   
  border = "white",      
  main   = "",
  xlab   = "FWB_Cog"
)

boxplot(
  data_clean$Cog,
  col    = "#99DCBD",  
  border = "black",    
  main   = "",        
  xlab   = "FWB_Cog", 
  horizontal = TRUE    
)

hist(
  data_clean$Behav, 
  breaks = 20,
  col    = "#99DCBD",    
  border = "white",    
  main   = "",
  xlab   = "FWB_Behav"
)

boxplot(
  data_clean$Behav,
  col    = "#99DCBD", 
  border = "black",   
  main   = "",         
  xlab   = "Behav", 
  horizontal = TRUE   
)

hist(
  data_clean$Emo, 
  breaks = 20,
  col    = "#99DCBD",   
  border = "white",     
  main   = "",
  xlab   = "FWB_Emo"
)

boxplot(
  data_clean$Emo,
  col    = "#99DCBD", 
  border = "black",   
  main   = "",        
  xlab   = "Emo",
  horizontal = TRUE    
)

3 Reliability

library(psych)

alpha(data_clean[, items_cog])
## 
## Reliability analysis   
## Call: alpha(x = data_clean[, items_cog])
## 
##   raw_alpha std.alpha G6(smc) average_r S/N   ase mean   sd median_r
##       0.82      0.82    0.81       0.4 4.7 0.013  3.8 0.76     0.39
## 
##     95% confidence boundaries 
##          lower alpha upper
## Feldt     0.79  0.82  0.85
## Duhachek  0.80  0.82  0.85
## 
##  Reliability if an item is dropped:
##       raw_alpha std.alpha G6(smc) average_r S/N alpha se  var.r med.r
## FWB03      0.80      0.80    0.77      0.40 3.9    0.015 0.0078  0.38
## FWB01      0.79      0.79    0.77      0.38 3.7    0.016 0.0053  0.38
## FWB05      0.79      0.79    0.77      0.39 3.8    0.016 0.0046  0.40
## FWB06      0.81      0.81    0.79      0.41 4.2    0.014 0.0069  0.41
## FWB07      0.78      0.79    0.76      0.38 3.7    0.016 0.0043  0.38
## FWB04      0.80      0.80    0.78      0.40 4.0    0.015 0.0074  0.38
## FWB10      0.82      0.82    0.80      0.43 4.6    0.014 0.0046  0.43
## 
##  Item statistics 
##         n raw.r std.r r.cor r.drop mean   sd
## FWB03 412  0.70  0.71  0.64   0.58  4.0 1.03
## FWB01 412  0.76  0.74  0.69   0.63  3.7 1.20
## FWB05 412  0.74  0.73  0.67   0.61  3.8 1.24
## FWB06 412  0.67  0.66  0.57   0.51  3.4 1.22
## FWB07 412  0.76  0.75  0.71   0.65  4.0 1.09
## FWB04 412  0.67  0.69  0.61   0.56  3.9 0.91
## FWB10 412  0.58  0.60  0.48   0.44  3.8 0.96
## 
## Non missing response frequency for each item
##          1    2    3    4    5 miss
## FWB03 0.03 0.08 0.13 0.42 0.34    0
## FWB01 0.07 0.13 0.14 0.39 0.28    0
## FWB05 0.06 0.14 0.16 0.28 0.36    0

## Omega 
## Call: omegah(m = m, nfactors = nfactors, fm = fm, key = key, flip = flip, 
##     digits = digits, title = title, sl = sl, labels = labels, 
##     plot = plot, n.obs = n.obs, rotate = rotate, Phi = Phi, option = option,

## Omega H asymptotic:    0.74 
## Omega Total            0.89

## Schmid Leiman Factor loadings greater than  0.2

## CBB05 0.27              0.39 0.23 0.23 0.77 0.32 1.84
## CBB07 0.56        0.38       0.48 0.48 0.52 0.66 1.90
## 
## With Sums of squares  of:
##    g  F1*  F2*  F3*   h2 
## 3.42 1.31 0.60 0.71 2.85 
## 
## general/max  1.2   max/min =   4.73
## mean percent general =  0.56    with sd =  0.11 and cv of  0.2 
## Explained Common Variance of the general factor =  0.57 
## 
## The degrees of freedom are 52  and the fit is  0.26 
## The number of observations was  412  with Chi Square =  105.5  with prob <  1.7e-05
## The root mean square of the residuals is  0.03 
## The df corrected root mean square of the residuals is  0.04
## RMSEA index =  0.05  and the 90 % confidence intervals are  0.036 0.064
## BIC =  -207.6
## 
## Compare this with the adequacy of just a general factor and no group factors
## The degrees of freedom for just the general factor are 77  and the fit is  1.11 
## The number of observations was  412  with Chi Square =  447.92  with prob <  1e-53
## The root mean square of the residuals is  0.11 
## The df corrected root mean square of the residuals is  0.12 
## 
## RMSEA index =  0.108  and the 90 % confidence intervals are  0.099 0.118
## BIC =  -15.7 
## 
## Measures of factor score adequacy             
##                                                  g  F1*   F2*   F3*
## Correlation of scores with factors            0.83 0.72  0.66  0.67
## Multiple R square of scores with factors      0.69 0.51  0.44  0.45
